question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted operator accountId string (e.g., '0.0.2.extra') that passes the split('\\.',3) length check but contains a non-numeric segment, causing Long.valueOf() to throw NumberFormatException and silently suppress record export startup, resulting in missing records on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the autoboxing comparison 'Long.valueOf(accountId[0]) == shard' (object reference equality instead of .equals()) to cause the shard/realm check to always evaluate false for shard values outside the Integer cache range (-128 to 127), silently skipping the early-return and triggering an incorrect accountId rewrite that corrupts the operator identity used for record export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user provide an operator accountId of '0.0.2' (matching DEFAULT_OPERATOR_ACCOUNT_ID) with a mismatched shard/realm in commonProperties, causing init() to silently rewrite the accountId to an unintended shard/realm combination, resulting in transactions being submitted under a wrong operator identity and records being incorrectly attributed on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a null mirrorNode configuration path so that getMirrorNode() falls back to network.getMirrorNode(), and if network is set to HederaNetwork.OTHER (which constructs a host of '.mirrornode.hedera.com'), cause all REST/gRPC subscriptions to target an invalid host, resulting in all records being missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set the network property to HederaNetwork.OTHER while leaving mirrorNode null, causing getMirrorNode() to return a MirrorNodeProperties with an empty-string host prefix ('.mirrornode.hedera.com'), which passes @NotBlank validation but resolves to an incorrect endpoint, causing all record subscriptions to fail silently and records to be missing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an operator accountId with leading/trailing whitespace (e.g., ' 0.0.2 ') that passes @NotBlank validation but causes split('\\\\.', 3) to produce segments with whitespace, making Long.valueOf() throw NumberFormatException and preventing the monitor from exporting any records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user provide an accountId of '0.0.' (trailing dot) that splits into ['0','0',''] with an empty third segment, passing the length==3 check but causing downstream AccountId.fromString() to fail, resulting in no records being published to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set the operator accountId to a value with a shard/realm that numerically matches commonProperties but uses a different account number, causing init() to return early without rewriting, and then submit transactions under an account that has no funds, causing all publish operations to fail and records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an operator accountId with a shard value of 128 or higher, exploiting the Long autoboxing cache boundary in 'Long.valueOf(accountId[0]) == shard', so the equality check returns false even when values are numerically equal, causing the accountId to be incorrectly rewritten and records exported under a wrong operator?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user configure network=TESTNET and leave mirrorNode null, then perform a DNS poisoning or BGP hijack against 'testnet.mirrornode.hedera.com' to redirect getMirrorNode()'s fallback endpoint to an attacker-controlled server, causing the monitor to export records to a fake mirror node and miss the legitimate one?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a null value for operator.getAccountId() (if bean injection allows null despite @NotBlank), causing accountId.split() to throw NullPointerException that is not caught, crashing the init() lifecycle and preventing any records from being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user provide an operator accountId of exactly '0.0.2' while commonProperties has shard=0 and realm=0, causing init() to return early without validation of the private key, allowing the default hardcoded private key to remain in use and all records to be signed with a publicly known key, making record authenticity on the mirror node unverifiable?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set mirrorNode to a non-null MirrorNodeProperties with an incorrect REST host, bypassing the Objects.requireNonNullElseGet fallback, so that getMirrorNode() returns the attacker-influenced endpoint and all record queries to the mirror node are directed to a wrong host, causing missing records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an operator accountId string containing Unicode digits (e.g., '０.０.２') that pass @NotBlank but cause Long.valueOf() to throw NumberFormatException, crashing init() and preventing the monitor from starting record export to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set the operator accountId to a value where accountId[0] and accountId[1] are valid longs matching shard/realm, but accountId[2] is a non-numeric string, causing init() to pass the equality check and return early, then fail later in AccountId.fromString() during client construction, resulting in no records being published?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user configure the network field to a value that causes HederaNetwork.getMirrorNode() to return a MirrorNodeProperties with port 80 instead of 443, causing MirrorNodeProperties.RestProperties.getBaseUrl() to use 'http://' instead of 'https://', exposing all record queries to the mirror node over plaintext and enabling MITM manipulation of exported records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user provide an operator accountId with a very large shard number (e.g., Long.MAX_VALUE) that overflows when formatted back via '%d.%d.%s', producing an incorrect accountId string that causes records to be exported under a wrong operator identity on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that init() only validates shard/realm of the operator accountId but not the account number segment (accountId[2]), allowing an arbitrary account number to be used as operator, causing records to be submitted from an unintended account and incorrectly attributed on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set mirrorNode.grpc.host to an empty string that passes @NotBlank only if whitespace is present, causing GrpcProperties.getEndpoint() to return ':443', which is an invalid gRPC endpoint, causing all gRPC subscriptions to fail and records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an operator accountId where the shard segment is '0' and realm segment is '0' but commonProperties.getShard() returns 0L and commonProperties.getRealm() returns 0L, and exploit the Long autoboxing cache (values 0 are cached) to make the == comparison succeed, but then provide a malformed account number that causes downstream failures in record export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set the network to HederaNetwork.OTHER and mirrorNode to null, causing getMirrorNode() to call OTHER.getMirrorNode() which sets host to '.mirrornode.hedera.com' (empty prefix), and then have the monitor silently connect to an invalid host, causing all records to be missing from the mirror node without any error thrown at startup?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user provide an operator accountId of '0.0.2' and set commonProperties shard to a value > 127, exploiting the Long autoboxing cache miss so that Long.valueOf('0') != 0L (as Long objects), causing the equality check to fail and init() to rewrite the accountId to an incorrect shard, resulting in records being exported under a wrong operator?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set the operator accountId to a string that splits into exactly 3 parts but where parts[0] or parts[1] are empty strings, causing Long.valueOf('') to throw NumberFormatException in init(), crashing the application context and preventing any records from being exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user configure mirrorNode.rest.port to 0 (which passes @Min(0) validation), causing getBaseUrl() to return 'http://host:0/api/v1', an invalid URL that causes all REST record queries to fail and records to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user set mirrorNode.rest.port to 443 but mirrorNode.rest.host to an IP address of an attacker-controlled server, causing getBaseUrl() to use 'https://' and direct all record queries to the attacker's server, resulting in records being exported to a fake mirror node?
